title: Proof, not promises
intro: Selected programmes with the numbers that matter. Every figure published here is verified with the customer concerned.

items.title: From Indonesian villages to American homes
items.quote: A fully certified rattan supply chain that pays artisans fairly and regenerates what it takes.
items.body: Launched in 2022 and operational since 2024, Sansico's integrated FSC rattan supply chain connects smallholder harvesters and village artisan groups to US retail through a single certified chain of custody. Developed in partnership with Target under the NEST handworker standard, the programme targets emission reduction, rural livelihoods and full material traceability — turning a traditional craft into a verified, repeatable export supply chain.

items.title: A rolling forecast that keeps toy shelves full
items.quote: Planning discipline turned a volatile category into a dependable supply line.
items.body: At PT Printec Perkasa, Sansico runs Mattel toy-packaging programmes on a rolling-forecast model: demand signals flow into materials planning and capacity allocation across batch cycles, with live-linked delivery tracking shared between the factory floor and the customer. The result is a packaging supply line that absorbs seasonal volatility without expediting costs or stockouts.

items.title: Four decades inside America's seasonal calendar
items.quote: Christmas does not move. Neither do our delivery dates.
items.body: For more than thirty years Sansico has produced gifting, card and seasonal programmes for Target, Walmart, Hallmark and US mass retail — engineering production across ten facilities to hit immovable seasonal windows, with C-TPAT/SCAN-audited supply-chain security and bonded-zone logistics keeping goods moving from Java to US distribution centres.
